It is not a direct anticoagulant like heparin or direct thrombin inhibitors such as dabigatran, which inhibit thrombin, the enzyme responsible for the final phase of blood clotting. <drug>valdecoxib</drug> was first marketed in 1998 under the trade name Bextra. It is a COX-2 inhibitor used to treat rheumatoid arthritis, psoriasis, and osteoarthritis. In 2004, Bextra was voluntarily removed from the market due to an increased risk of heart attack and stroke. In 2005, Bextra was re-launched under the name Celebrex. Celebrex is now approved for the treatment of acute pain, osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, and juvenile idiopathic arthritis.
